title: Cleisostoma belophorum
intro: Cleisostoma belophorum, commonly known as the "Belophorum Orchid," is a type of flowering orchid native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, Cleisostoma belophorum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its purported health benefits. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cleisostoma belophorum is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and is also used in the form of a paste or a poultice to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Cleisostoma belophorum is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, specifically within the monocot division. The genus Cleisostoma is further grouped under the subfamily Epidendroideae, which comprises a vast array of orchid species. As a member of the Epidendroideae subfamily, Cleisostoma belophorum shares characteristics with other orchids, such as the presence of modified leaves and a unique flower structure. This classification system helps to organize and understand the relationships among different plant species within the orchid family.
